title: When Recalling Visuals: - Focus on Details: Try to Actively Recall Both the Foreground
subtitle: Avoid Boundary Extension Errors
description:
When recalling visuals:
- Focus on details: Try to actively recall both the foreground and the background.
- Verify: Revisit the original image or scene to compare your memory.
- Practice recall: Test yourself on visual memories to sharpen your accuracy.

Example: After seeing a photo, draw it from memory and then compare it to the original.

howTo.title: Define the metric baseline
howTo.summary: Identify your current ability to recall visual details. Take a sample image, view it for 30 seconds, and then recall what you remember. Count the correct contextual anchors and note any errors.

howTo.title: Run the 3-day experiment
howTo.summary: Practice recalling visuals daily for three days. Use different images, focusing on foreground, anchors, and relations. Log your observations and compare your recall accuracy each day.

howTo.title: Implement timed recalls
howTo.summary: Set a timer for 30 seconds to view an image, followed by 60 seconds for recall. This structure helps improve focus and accuracy in memory retention.

howTo.title: Review and adjust strategies
howTo.summary: After the experiment, analyze your results. Identify which strategies improved your recall and adjust your practice methods accordingly for better outcomes.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results and reflections in the Brali LifeOS app to track your progress and maintain your practice. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/avoid-boundary-extension-errors/.
